projects.name: Site Survey Tool
projects.next_milestone: Plan + timeline drafted (planning work happens in that project; timeline TBD until plan exists)
projects.notes: Discovery phase: researching comparable market tools and documenting the current highly manual process before designing a replacement. TopStock Site Survey Documentation session held 6/11. Schema design underway — see design_decisions.

design_decisions.title: Spatial hierarchy
design_decisions.detail: Locked hierarchy: Retailer → Store → Floor → Zone → optional Aisle → Section (= bay). Shelf-level addressing is optional. Shelf indexing convention: bottom shelf = 1, stored as an explicit attribute (not inferred).

design_decisions.title: Three-tier data-model north-star
design_decisions.detail: Data model is three layers: survey (what's physically there) → requirement (what a product needs) → deployment plan (how a product maps onto the surveyed space). Anchor-tag placement is deployment-layer interpretation and is explicitly out of survey capture scope.

design_decisions.title: Product-agnostic survey schema
design_decisions.detail: Survey schema is deliberately product-agnostic as a forcing function — it captures the space, not any one product's needs. Product-specific requirements are deferred to an additive layer that references the survey rather than being baked into capture.

design_decisions.title: Three-bucket data model (declared / observed / interpreted)
design_decisions.detail: Every captured value sorts into one of three buckets: declared (asserted by a human up front), observed (raw sensor/marker sighting), or interpreted (derived/reconciled). Bucket assignment decided by a field-sort test. Multi-pass fusion keeps ALL passes as immutable observations and reconciles them into a single interpretation — observations are never overwritten.

design_decisions.title: Schema assumption ledger A1–A5
design_decisions.detail: Ledger commits the schema SHAPE while explicitly parking the algorithms: A1 marker sightings, A2 frame-to-frame alignment, A3 segmented sessions, A4 sibling-reading interpretations, A5 fusion. Each assumption fixes what the schema must hold without committing to how it's computed.

design_decisions.title: Two-marker break-marker workflow
design_decisions.detail: Session resume / re-localization uses a two-marker break-marker workflow (kit decals + training). De-risks Decision 4 versioning by reusing the same alignment mechanism against a permanent anchor.

changelog.entry: Tracker initialized. In planning; no committed dates yet.
Locked schema Decision 1 — spatial hierarchy: Retailer→Store→Floor→Zone→optional Aisle→Section(=bay), optional shelf-level addressing, bottom=1 shelf indexing stored as an attribute.
Defined three-tier data-model north-star (survey → requirement → deployment plan); anchor-tag placement scoped as deployment-layer interpretation, out of capture scope.
Established product-agnostic survey schema as a deliberate forcing function; product requirements deferred to an additive layer referencing the survey.

Locked schema Decision 2 — three-bucket model (declared/observed/interpreted) sorted by field-sort test; multi-pass fusion keeps all passes as immutable observations, reconciles into one interpretation.
Established schema assumption ledger A1–A5 (marker sightings, frame-to-frame alignment, segmented sessions, sibling-reading interpretations, fusion) — commits schema shape while parking algorithms.
Adopted two-marker break-marker workflow for session resume/re-localization (kit decals + training); de-risks Decision 4 versioning via same alignment mechanism with permanent anchor.

carry_forwards.item: Definitive answer on camera-behind-glass scope (Sainsbury's Captana pilot)
carry_forwards.rule: Carries forward weekly until resolved. Cannot be deleted — only resolved with date + resolution note.
Carries forward weekly until resolved. Cannot be deleted — only resolved with date + resolution note.

carry_forwards.item: Blink ID reliability escalation — random, non-reproducible ESL misses with no command acknowledgement, retry, or event-level traceability
carry_forwards.rule: Active escalation. Carries forward weekly until resolved.

recurring_deliverables.name: Weekly leadership slide
recurring_deliverables.standard: Thaddeus directive: single slide, max 200 words, delete unused boxes, relevance over completeness

recurring_deliverables.name: Weekly recap email
recurring_deliverables.standard: Subject format: Weekly Recap | [Date] | Product Implementation. SharePoint links left as [LINK] placeholders.

operating_principles: Deployment-to-results: every performance result tied to a specific deployment config (this config = this performance = this cost)
Unresolved gaps carry forward every week without exception, flagged until nauseating
